items.blurb: Gold-kissed Year of the Horse stickers that make January planner spreads feel like a tiny festival. Once they gallop off, they're gone for good.
Ring in 2026 the kawaii way: a foil-accented sheet of little lucky horses and New Year charms, made in Japan and gone once the year begins.
Petite lucky-horse stickers with a shimmery foil finish, sized just right for tucking good fortune into every corner of your 2026 planner.
Twelve tiny pup-covered mini calendars to stick on your laptop, phone, or planner — the friendliest way to know what day it is.
A year of little cats, one mini calendar per month, ready to perch on your laptop lid and quietly judge your schedule.
Twelve peel-and-stick mini calendars, each dressed for its month — a small seasonal mood board for wherever your eyes land most.
Little critters cuddling desserts across twelve stick-anywhere monthly calendars. A dangerously cute way to track deadlines.
Tocorocomugi's soft illustrated world shrunk into twelve removable monthly calendars — planner, phone, and laptop all get a glow-up.
Artist Shoichiro Tobimatsu turns the whole of 2026 into twelve charming stick-on mini calendars for your most-glanced-at surfaces.
An 80s-Harajuku homage pressed into one tiny can badge by The Superior Labor — event-only, one per person, pure pop-culture nostalgia.
The Superior Labor's retro Harajuku daydream in badge form: limited, collectible, and just the thing for a tote strap or jacket lapel.
A pocket-sized love letter to 1980s Harajuku from The Superior Labor. Pin it on and carry a little Tokyo street style everywhere.
Event-exclusive and gone fast: The Superior Labor's back-to-the-80s badge brings vintage Harajuku flair to bags, jackets, and pinboards.
Two travel-happy badges from illustrator eric, made only for the Hello, Harajuku event. Pin one to your bag, gift its twin to a travel buddy.
Illustrator eric bottles wanderlust into one sturdy vinyl sticker — a limited 80s-Harajuku souvenir for laptops, luggage, and journals.
A retro music moment from illustrator eric, printed on durable vinyl for the event only. Stick it where your playlists live.
eric's gentle nudge to savour life's little cafe moments, rendered on a limited-run vinyl sticker with 80s Harajuku soul.
Roll back to 1980s Harajuku with eric's roller-skate vinyl sticker — an event exclusive with serious retro-cute momentum.
Two acrylic charms of eric's cafe-daydream art, made only for the Hello, Harajuku event — one for your keys, one for a lucky friend.
A duo of travel-themed acrylic keychains by eric, limited to the Harajuku event. Clip on some wanderlust before your next trip.
eric celebrates life's small joys in two event-only acrylic charms — sweetly retro, endlessly clippable, impossible to pick just one.
Retro roller skates by illustrator eric, doubled into a limited acrylic keychain set that brings 80s Harajuku energy to any bag.
A whole A6 sheet of eric's 80s-Harajuku doodles, made just for the event — enough retro-cute stickers to redecorate an entire journal.
Collage artist Miki Tamura layers torn-paper pouches into a washi tape that means 'variety' — and delivers it, inch by lovely inch.
Miki Tamura's torn-washi collage magic, wound onto a roll of favourite little things for journal margins that deserve better.
A parade of collaged donuts by artist Miki Tamura — zero calories, maximum charm, and your planner suddenly smells like a bakery.
Miki Tamura's layered-paper fashion sketches on a roll — like trimming your journal with pages from a very stylish tiny wardrobe.
Sunlit collage scenes from Miki Tamura, torn and layered into a washi tape that makes any spread feel like a good-weather morning.
A collaged girl in a sunshine-yellow skirt by Miki Tamura — frame it, mail it, or prop it on a shelf for instant gallery-wall warmth.
Step into Miki Tamura's imaginary dream cafe via one layered-washi postcard — cozy enough to hang above your actual coffee corner.
Everyday moments, collaged from tiny torn washi pieces by Miki Tamura — the gentlest way to mark ordinary days as special.
Miki Tamura's torn-paper ode to favourite things, postcard-sized — the kind of mail people pin up instead of recycling.
Miki Tamura's 2026 calendar reads like a tiny storybook: monthly collage pages slotted into a little wooden stand for your desk.
Patchworked paper scraps become quiet poetry on Yohaku's Tsugihagi tape — instant vintage texture for journal spreads and snail mail.
Yohaku's Gray tape proves neutrals can be soulful: soft smudges of old paper in misty tones for the minimalist journaler.
Scribbled fragments and old notepaper, collaged by Yohaku into a tape that makes every page look pleasantly well-loved.
Nine chunky vinyl stickers by artist Ame, mixing Texas pride with Japanese cuteness for a Houston pop-up — pick your favourite crossover.
The essential Yohaku roll: layered antique-paper fragments ready to give any journal that carefully-undone collage look.
Yohaku muses on tomorrow in gentle torn-paper layers — a washi tape for hopeful planner pages and new-chapter journaling.
A melody you can stick down: Yohaku's Secret Song tape hums with soft collaged fragments made for late-night journaling.
A small blue bird flits through Yohaku's collaged paper scraps — quiet, lucky-feeling trim for letters and journal edges.
Hushed whites and wintry paper layers: Yohaku's Snowy Morning tape wraps your pages in that first-snow kind of quiet.
Yohaku packs a paper picnic — sunny collaged fragments that turn any journal spread into a blanket-on-the-grass afternoon.
Calm, considered scraps in perfect equilibrium — Yohaku's Balance tape is meditation you can peel and stick.
Yohaku goes greyscale: inky fragments and pale paper in one chic monochrome roll for understated, gallery-ish journal pages.
Faded tickets and time-softened scraps, collaged by Yohaku into a tape that makes brand-new pages feel like found treasures.
Yohaku's World tape wanders through map-like paper fragments — a passport stamp for journals with itchy feet.
Little rooftops and streets emerge from Yohaku's collaged scraps — a sleepy paper town to run along the bottom of your pages.
Vintage type and stray numerals, rescued by Yohaku into a tape that gives journals that old-print-shop character.
Yohaku makes even schedules beautiful: collaged planner fragments that dress up to-do lists you might actually finish.
Motifs that circle back like a refrain — Yohaku's Rondo tape loops soft paper fragments into a gentle visual rhythm.
Envelope edges and handwritten whispers: Yohaku's Letter tape is made for sealing snail mail with quiet sentiment.
Dusky florals bloom across Yohaku's Evening Primrose tape — a twilight garden pressed into torn-paper layers.
Yohaku scatters paper moons and stars across this dreamy roll — nightsky journaling trim with a soft vintage glow.
A one-person adventure in collage form: Yohaku's Solo Travel tape is for trip journals and the brave little plans inside them.
Yohaku distils yesteryear into faded fragments and soft edges — a nostalgia-soaked border for scrapbooks and keepsake pages.
Gentle weather, gentle tape: Yohaku's Fine and Mild layers calm, sun-warmed scraps for the softest journal days.
You can almost feel the breeze in Yohaku's Gentle Wind — airy paper fragments drifting across one very calming roll.
Collaged tulips in soft antique tones — Yohaku's springtime roll plants a tiny flowerbed along your journal margins.
First-day-of-the-trip energy, collaged from old paper by Yohaku — perfect for fresh notebooks and brave beginnings.
Climbing clematis blooms wind through Yohaku's torn-paper layers — a trellis of quiet florals for your prettiest pages.
Ripples rendered in paper: Yohaku's Water Surface tape brings a still-pond calm to journals that need a deep breath.
An artist's palette in scrap form — Yohaku daubs soft colour fragments along one roll for creative, painterly spreads.
Quiet inner-voice energy: Yohaku's Monologue tape lines diary pages with soft, thoughtful paper fragments.
Departure-board daydreams collaged by Yohaku — boarding-pass fragments for travel journals waiting on their next stamp.
Yohaku catches starlight and its shadow in layered paper — a subtly celestial trim for dreamy nighttime journaling.
Sweet violas pressed into Yohaku's signature collage — a pocket-sized flowerpatch that never wilts on the page.
A pressed-flower archive on a roll: Yohaku's Herbarium tape scatters botanical fragments like pages from an old field journal.
Lazy golden-hour scraps, collaged by Yohaku into a tape that tastes faintly of tea breaks and unhurried journaling.
Hope in paper form — Yohaku's Tomorrow's Light layers pale, sunrise-soft fragments for optimistic new pages.
New-growth greens flicker through Yohaku's Fresh Leaf tape — springtime renewal for planners turning over a new leaf.
Russet scraps and falling-leaf fragments: Yohaku bottles one perfect autumn day into a cozy roll of washi.
Half-remembered shapes drift through Yohaku's In a Dream tape — hazy collage layers for your most wistful spreads.
Yohaku translates rainfall into soft grey-blue paper fragments — a cozy, pitter-patter border for rainy-day journaling.
Wander a torn-paper forest in fall colours — Yohaku's Autumn Forest tape carpets your pages in quiet amber leaves.
Tiny stitched motifs run through Yohaku's collage like grandma's sewing box — handmade warmth in peel-and-stick form.
Still water and hidden shores, collaged by Yohaku into a tape that feels like finding a quiet place no one else knows.
Open skies torn from old paper — Yohaku's Sky and Wind roll lets a little fresh air into cramped planner weeks.
Yohaku plants a spring garden in collage: budding paper blooms that make journal margins feel freshly watered.
Warm scraps soaked in paper sunshine — Yohaku's Sunlight tape pours a soft golden glow along every edge it touches.
